data.title: Chapter 1.1: Heart Structure
data.description: This chapter provides a detailed overview of the anatomical structure of the human heart, including its chambers, valves, major blood vessels, and surrounding tissues. Trainees will understand how each part of the heart contributes to blood circulation and overall cardiac function. The chapter also introduces the relationship between cardiac anatomy and pacemaker placement, forming the basis for further learning in cardiac electrophysiology and device therapy.

data.title: Chapter 1.2: Valves and Their Functions
data.description: This chapter focuses on the four major heart valves — the tricuspid, pulmonary, mitral, and aortic valves — and their role in maintaining unidirectional blood flow through the heart. Trainees will learn how valves open and close during the cardiac cycle, how they support efficient circulation, and how valve dysfunction can affect cardiac performance. The chapter also introduces the clinical importance of valve anatomy in cardiac procedures and pacemaker-related interventions.

data.title: Chapter 1.3: Heart Mechanical Function
data.description: This chapter explains the mechanical functioning of the heart as a pumping organ responsible for circulating blood throughout the body. Trainees will learn about the cardiac cycle, including systole and diastole, ventricular contraction, blood ejection, and chamber relaxation. The chapter also covers how coordinated mechanical activity supports effective circulation and how abnormalities in heart function can influence cardiac rhythm management and pacemaker therapy.
